ocr.text: The first table presents various questionnaires related to haemophilia across different age groups and measures, including the
headers Age Group, Type of Measures*, Name of questionnaire, Instrument, Dimension/subscales, N.°of Items**, Way of
administration, Reliability, Validity in Haemophilia Examined, N° of languages, and Use in haemophilia Research.

Age Group  Type of Measures*  Name of questionnaire  Instrument  Dimension/subscales  N.°of Items**  Way of administration  Reliability  Validity in Haemophilia Examined  N° of languages  Use in haemophilia Research

Children  HRQoL  Haemo-QoL  Haemophilia -specific QoL Questionnaire for children patients and parents  8-12 (physical health, feeling, view of yourself, family, friends, perceived support, others, sports and school, dealing with haemophilia, treatment, future, relationships, global health)  I: 21 for children aged 4-7 II: 64 for children aged 8-12 III: 77 for adolescents aged 13-16  Self/Proxy  α = 0.85-0.91 (for the different age group versions)  yes  40  Epidemiological study (describing the quality of life of patients with reference to other chronic conditions), Clinical trials (to evaluate the potential benefits of different treatment regimes), Quality assurance (identifying the quality of care perceived by patients, e.g., in Haemophilia Comprehensive Care Centres (HCCC), Health-economic studies (assessing costs and benefits of haemophilia treatment with regard to economic indicators) and Routine treatment

Adults  Haem-A-QoL  Disease-specific questionnaire for adults patients  10 (physical health, feelings, view of yourself, sport and leisure, work and school, dealing with haemophilia, treatment, future, family planning, partnership and sexuality)  46  Self  α = 0.74-0.88  yes  32

Medtap (Haemo-QoL-A)  Haemophilia-specific QoL questionnaire for adults  4 (day-to-day activities, mood and feelings, work or school life, family life -social life, haemophilia treatment)  41  Self  α = 0.75-0.95  yes  20

TS  Hemo-SatA  Haemophilia Treatment satisfaction Question- naire  6 (Ease and convenience, Efficacy Burden, Specialist/nurses, Centre/Hospital, General satisfaction with your treatment  34  Self  α = 0.71-0.95  yes  24  To evaluate patients' therapy and experience, in follow-up analysis

Functioning  HEP-Test Q  Subjective assessment questionnaire of the effects of physical functioning in adult with haemophilia  5 (physical status, mobility strength-coordination, endurance, body perception)  25 + 1  Self  α = 0.70-0.90  yes  3  Evaluation of patients' daily activities or functioning, Follow-up analysis, Rehabilitation Programs
Test (interaction)  β(SE)  p-Value
Serial digit learning (AG × age)
Symbol-digit latency (AG × education)
For Children, the HRQoL measure Haemo-QoL encompasses a haemophilia-specific QoL questionnaire for children patients
and parents with dimensions/subscales covering 12 aspects of life, consisting of 21 items for ages 4-7, 64 for ages 8-12, and 77
for adolescents aged 13-16, administered via Self/Proxy, achieving a reliability of α = 0.85-0.91, with validity confirmed in
haemophilia, available in 40 languages, and used in various research contexts. For Adults, the Haem-A-QoL questionnaire
includes 10 dimensions, 46 items, and a reliability of α = 0.74-0.88, available in 32 languages. The questionnaire Medtap
(Haemo-QoL-A) measures 4 dimensions with 41 items and a reliability of α = 0.75-0.95 in 20 languages. The questionnaire
Hemo-SatA evaluates treatment satisfaction with 6 dimensions, 34 items, and a reliability of α = 0.71-0.95 in 24 languages,
while the HEP-Test Q assesses physical functioning with 5 dimensions, 26 items, and a reliability of α = 0.70-0.90 in 3
languages. The second table details interaction tests, featuring headers Test (interaction), β(SE), and p-Value, reporting results
for Serial digit learning (AG × age) with Non-AG at 0.26 (0.17), p = 0.13, and AG at −0.16 (0.12), p = 0.20; Symbol-digit
latency (AG × education) with Non-AG at −90.87 (40.17), p = 0.03, and AG at −197.52 (33.12), p < 0.01.
